message:
fix: timer wheel slot navigation now wraps around and scans all levels

Fix next_occupied_before_wrap → next_occupied_distance to support full
wrap-around scanning in the timer wheel bitmap. Previously, the function
only scanned forward from cursor+1 to slot 255 and returned None if no
occupied slot was found in that range. This meant timers in slots 0..cursor
were invisible to the skip optimization, causing unnecessary tick-by-tick
advancement through empty slots until the next cascade boundary.

Changes to WheelLevel::next_occupied_distance:
- After scanning cursor+1..255, now wraps to scan 0..cursor, returning
  the full circular distance (SLOTS_PER_LEVEL - cursor + found).
- Early return None when all bitmap words are zero (common case).
- Cursor-at-255 now correctly returns Some(1) when slot 0 is occupied,
  instead of None.

Changes to TimerWheel::next_skip_tick:
- Now scans all levels (not just level 0) with proper resolution scaling
  (r_i *= SLOTS_PER_LEVEL for each level), finding the globally nearest
  occupied slot across the entire wheel hierarchy.
- After multi-tick skips, calls realign_cursors_to_current_tick() instead
  of manually adjusting only the level-0 cursor, which keeps all level
  cursors consistent.

Updated tests:
- bitmap_next_occupied_distance: slot 5 (behind cursor 10) now returns
  Some(251) instead of None; renamed from bitmap_next_occupied_before_wrap.
- bitmap_cursor_at_255_wraps: slot 0 with cursor 255 returns Some(1);
  renamed from bitmap_cursor_at_255_returns_none.
- bitmap_next_occupied_at_word_boundary: updated to use new function name.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
